description: A sustainability specialist at a large data center operator in northern Nevada is evaluating renewable energy procurement options. Model a proposed 15 MW binary-cycle (conversion_type 0) hydrothermal (resource_type 0) plant with a 175°C resource using PySAM and the LCOE Calculator financial model. Use a Nevada or western US TMY file for the weather data, with the directory path located in /home/ga/.SAM/solar_resource_dir.txt. Write the Python modeling script to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/geothermal_model.py and save the simulation results to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/geothermal_results.json. The JSON output must contain the following keys: resource_temp_c, conversion_type, nameplate_kw, annual_energy_kwh, capacity_factor, lcoe_cents_per_kwh, num_wells, and weather_file.
